perturbation.original_text: Affiliate agrees not to sell commercial time to or for the benefit of direct competitors of the Service (e.g., music video networks carried by MVPDs such as MTV, VH1 and Fuse). All Local Advertising shall comply with the pertinent Station's generally applicable broadcast standards and Affiliate shall be solely responsible for all Local Advertising and all liabilities associated therewith, including insertion, trafficking, billing and collection activities relating to the Local Advertising and for the content of the material inserted into the Local Advertising.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate agrees not to sell commercial time to or for the benefit of direct competitors of the Service (e.g., music video networks carried by MVPDs such as MTV, VH1 and Fuse). However, Affiliate has the full autonomy to sell commercial time to any entity, even direct competitors, if it deems commercially viable. All Local Advertising shall comply with the pertinent Station's generally applicable broadcast standards and Affiliate shall be solely responsible for all Local Advertising and all liabilities associated therewith, including insertion, trafficking, billing and collection activities relating to the Local Advertising and for the content of the material inserted into the Local Advertising.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'However, Affiliate has the full autonomy to sell commercial time to any entity, even direct competitors, if it deems commercially viable' directly contradicts the initial sentence that prevents Affiliate from selling commercial time to competitors of the Service. This introduction creates an internal contradiction, potentially leading to disputes over whether Affiliate can or cannot sell to competitors of the service, specifically if a previous clause in the text prohibits that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Network shall provide to each Station that transmits the Service for local advertising sales, public service announcements, newsbreaks, station-produced vignettes or promotion one (1) minute of commercial announcement time per hour ("Local Advertising"), normally at the same approximate time each hour of the broadcast day. Affiliate shall have the right to retain for itself all the proceeds derived from the sale of Local Advertising. Affiliate agrees not to sell commercial time to or for the benefit of direct competitors of the Service (e.g., music video networks carried by MVPDs such as MTV, VH1 and Fuse). However, Affiliate has the full autonomy to sell commercial time to any entity, even direct competitors, if it deems commercially viable. All Local Advertising shall comply with the pertinent Station's generally applicable broadcast standards and Affiliate shall be solely responsible for all Local Advertising and all liabilities associated therewith, including insertion, trafficking, billing and collection activities relating to the Local Advertising and for the content of the material inserted into the Local Advertising.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. Initially, the 'Affiliate agrees not to sell commercial time to or for the benefit of direct competitors'. The addition of 'However, Affiliate has the full autonomy to sell commercial time to any entity, even direct competitors, if it deems commercially viable' directly opposes the preceding statement. This creates a clear in-text contradiction, as it both prohibits and permits the same action, rendering the clause ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, and fitting the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each Station agrees to transmit the Service on a full-time basis 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, except in cases of force majeure, emergency broadcasts, when a Station's Primary Feed is not being transmitted, as provided in 4(c) above, or when a Station must stop broadcasting for maintenance or repairs. Each Station will provide Network with up to 5.0 mbps, but, at all times, not less than 2.0 mbps, for this purpose, except as required in infrequent and exceptional circumstances resulting from a Station's carriage of the primary television network with which such Station is affiliated with regard to its Primary Feed (e.g., ABC, CBS, NBC and Fox). Except for a Station's Local Advertising Time and Local Programming, station identification messages, and as except provided in 4(c) and 4(e) above, each Station shall transmit the Service without alteration, editing or delay.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Station agrees to transmit the Service on a best-effort basis, aiming for 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, but is not obligated to do so at any given time. Each Station will provide Network with up to 5.0 mbps, but, at all times, not less than 2.0 mbps, for this purpose, except as required in infrequent and exceptional circumstances resulting from a Station's carriage of the primary television network with which such Station is affiliated with regard to its Primary Feed (e.g., ABC, CBS, NBC and Fox). Except for a Station's Local Advertising Time and Local Programming, station identification messages, and as except provided in 4(c) and 4(e) above, each Station shall transmit the Service without alteration, editing or delay.
perturbation.explanation: The modification from 'full-time basis' to 'best-effort basis, aiming for 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, but is not obligated to do so at any given time' creates a contradiction with the initial agreement for continuous service. While the original text establishes an expectation of consistent transmission, the added flexibility introduces uncertainty about adherence to that agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction with the rest of the agreement, which bases revenue sharing and promotional obligations on the premise of full-time broadcasting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the terms and conditions set forth herein, Network shall pay Affiliate (i) the Affiliate Advertising Share, and (ii) the Affiliate Transactional Share, each as provided in Exhibit D.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that each station agrees to transmit service on a full-time basis while the changed text modifies this to a 'best-effort basis, aiming for 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, but is not obligated to do so at any given time.' This change introduces a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'Network shall pay Affiliate (i) the Affiliate Advertising Share, and (ii) the Affiliate Transactional Share, each as provided in Exhibit D' based on the premise of full-time broadcasting. By removing the full-time obligation and introducing a best-effort clause, there is now a significant in-text contradiction in the contract that makes the terms of revenue sharing ambigious, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
